title: Senior Data Analyst
description: Architecting automated BI dashboards and ETL pipelines that turn chaotic student demographic data into something executive leadership can actually act on. Doing Propensity Score Matching to evaluate program outcomes and serving as data advisor to the DEIA committee.

title: Data Analyst
description: Started here digging into student retention datasets with SQL and Python. Built ETL pipelines that took hours of manual work down to minutes and surfaced insights that contributed to a 200% improvement in retention rates.

title: Lead Frontend Web Developer
description: Led a frontend team working on a Next.js/React app, set up TypeScript standards and CI/CD pipelines, and integrated Mixpanel + Google Analytics for data-driven A/B testing. Boosted conversion rates by 15% and cut technical debt by 30% by modernizing a legacy codebase. Also fixed more bugs than I'd like to admit.

title: Full Stack Engineer
description: Built a custom helpdesk dashboard connecting multiple third-party APIs through a Prisma/Postgres backend. Designed a smart-search feature with tag-based filtering that made digging through old tickets actually bearable. The 'find similar tickets' feature was the most useful thing I shipped that year. Allowing helpdesk to go through the mess of IT and understanding problems with limited context

title: Research Student
description: Designed evolutionary algorithms for optimizing wildfire sensor node placement across Southern California terrain — accounting for topography, sensor range, and coverage overlap. Co-presented at SCCUR and was the main author at NCUR

title: B.S. Computer Science
description: Learned data structures, algorithms, and how to survive compilers and OS. Honestly still don't know how I did it. Graduated with a research background in GIS and evolutionary computation. The theory from class actually showed up in the wildfire research, which was a nice surprise.
